revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 菟狭津彦命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[上古#日本|上古]]・[[弥生時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 宇沙都比古、菟狭津彦、宇佐都彦命、宇佐津彦命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 初代[[宇佐国造]] | 主君 = [[神武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[宇佐氏]] | 父母 = 父：天三降命 | 兄弟 = 宇佐津媛命 | 妻 = | 子 = 常津彦命 | 特記事項 = }} '''菟狭津彦命'''（うさつひこのみこと、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[宇佐国造]]'''。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では[[豊国]]の[[宇佐郡|宇沙]]に、『[[日本書紀]]』では[[筑紫国]]の菟狭に入った人物で、[[比売大神]]に奉仕したとされている{{efn|比売大神が曾祖母の[[天照大神]]であるか、次代の[[宗像三女神]]であるかは不明。}}。
{{quote|宇佐郡 宇沙都彦命（忠君）（敬神）（大義名分）
[[高皇産霊尊|高皇産令命]]の孫[[天御降命]]の子で宇佐島に座した。比売大神に奉仕したが 神武天皇東征の途次 宇佐に到り座せし一時 一柱騰宮を建てて御養を奉った。此れ即ち[[宇佐氏]]の祖で子孫は[[宮成氏|宮成]]、[[到津氏|到津]]両[[男爵]]家を始め数百家に反映してゐる。|『修身科郷土資料集成』{{sfn|大分県教育会|1936}}}}
『日本書紀』では、神武天皇が[[神武東征|東征]]し、中洲（[[大和国]]）をめざして[[国津神|国神]]の[[椎根津彦|珍彦]]を海導者として[[筑紫国]]の[[宇佐郡|菟狭]]に上陸したとき、[[宇佐国造]]の祖である菟狭津彦と[[菟狭津媛]]が{{efn|[[ヒメヒコ制]]による2首長であろう。}}、菟狭川の川上に[[一柱騰宮]]（あしひとつ あがりのみや）を造営して饗応の奉仕をした。そこで神武天皇は勅をして、菟狭津媛を待臣の[[天種子命]]（[[中臣氏]]遠祖）に娶らせたとされる。
== 系譜 == {{see |宇佐氏}} [[高皇産霊尊]]（たかみむすび）の孫・[[天三降命]]（[[天活玉命]]の子）の子とされるが、「宮成家譜」に天三降命は「天孫天降日向之時供奉」とあり、実際には天三降命は菟狭津彦命の祖父と見られる<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「第1章 天神系氏族 第11節 宇佐氏族 1宇佐公、宇佐宿祢」『古代氏族系譜集成』中巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、978頁。</ref>。子に常津彦命がいるとされる。
妹の菟狭津媛命は[[天種子命]]の妻となり、[[中臣氏|中臣連]]の祖である[[宇佐津臣命]]を生んだ。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *{{anchors|大分県教育会}}{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県教育会| translator=| title=[{{NDLDC|918478/45}} 修身科郷土資料集成]| page=73| publisher=大分県教育会| location =大分県| year=1936| isbn=| ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[国造]] *『[[金烏玉兎集|簠簋内伝 金烏玉兎集]]』 * [[漢四郡]]（[[玄菟郡]]、[[楽浪郡]]、[[真番郡]]、[[臨屯郡]]）
{{神道 横}} {{日本神話}} {{Normdaten}} {{デフォルトソート:うさつひこのみこと}} [[category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:豊国の人物]] [[Category:豊前国の人物]] [[Category:大分県の歴史]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 菟狭津彦命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[上古#日本|上古]]・[[弥生時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 宇沙都比古、菟狭津彦、宇佐都彦命、宇佐津彦命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 初代[[宇佐国造]] | 主君 = [[神武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[宇佐氏]] | 父母 = 父：天三降命 | 兄弟 = 宇佐津媛命 | 妻 = | 子 = 常津彦命 | 特記事項 = }} '''菟狭津彦命'''（うさつひこのみこと、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[宇佐国造]]'''。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では'''宇沙都比古'''、『[[日本書紀]]』、「[[皇孫本紀]]」では'''菟狭津彦'''、「国造本紀」では'''宇佐都彦命'''、[[中臣氏|中臣氏族]]の各種系図<ref>[[中田憲信]]「赤堀」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>[[鈴木真年]]「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>中田憲信「兼松」『諸系譜』第二十四冊。</ref><ref name="keifu">中田憲信「荒木家系譜」『諸系譜』第冊。</ref>では'''宇佐津彦命'''と表記される。
『[[古事記]]』では[[豊国]]の[[宇佐郡|宇沙]]に、『[[日本書紀]]』では[[筑紫国]]の菟狭にいた人物とされ、菟狭津媛命と共に兄妹で登場する。
『日本書紀』では、[[神武天皇]]が[[神武東征|東征]]し、中洲（[[大和国]]）をめざして[[国津神|国神]]の[[椎根津彦|珍彦]]を海導者として[[筑紫国]]の[[宇佐郡|菟狭]]に上陸したとき、[[宇佐国造|菟狭国造]]の祖である菟狭津彦と[[菟狭津媛]]がおり、[[駅館川|菟狭川]]の川上に一柱騰宮（あしひとつ あがりのみや）を造営して饗応の奉仕をした。そこで神武天皇は勅をして、妹の菟狭津媛を待臣の[[天種子命]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]遠祖）に娶らせたとされる。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』、『古事記』においても同様の伝承を記すが、『古事記』では天種子命に関する記述がない。
{{quote|宇佐郡 宇沙都彦命（忠君）（敬神）（大義名分）
[[高皇産霊尊|高皇産令命]]の孫[[天三降命|天御降命]]の子で宇佐島に座した。比売大神に奉仕したが 神武天皇東征の途次 宇佐に到り座せし一時 一柱騰宮を建てて御養を奉った。此れ即ち[[宇佐氏]]の祖で子孫は[[宮成氏|宮成]]、[[到津氏|到津]]両[[男爵]]家を始め数百家に反映してゐる。|『修身科郷土資料集成』{{sfn|大分県教育会|1936}}}}
== 系譜 == {{see |宇佐氏}} [[高皇産霊尊]]（たかみむすび）の孫・[[天三降命]]（[[天活玉命]]の子）の子とされるが、「宮成家譜」に天三降命は「天孫天降日向之時供奉」とあり、実際には天三降命は菟狭津彦命の祖父と見られる<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「第1章 天神系氏族 第11節 宇佐氏族 1宇佐公、宇佐宿祢」『古代氏族系譜集成』中巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、978頁。</ref>。子に常津彦命がいるとされる。
妹の菟狭津媛命は[[天種子命]]の妻となり、[[中臣氏|中臣連]]の祖である[[宇佐津臣命]]を生んだ。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *{{anchors|大分県教育会}}{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県教育会| translator=| title=[{{NDLDC|918478/45}} 修身科郷土資料集成]| page=73| publisher=大分県教育会| location =大分県| year=1936| isbn=| ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[国造]] *『[[金烏玉兎集|簠簋内伝 金烏玉兎集]]』 * [[漢四郡]]（[[玄菟郡]]、[[楽浪郡]]、[[真番郡]]、[[臨屯郡]]）

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 菟狭津彦命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[上古#日本|上古]]・[[弥生時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 宇沙都比古、菟狭津彦、宇佐都彦命、宇佐津彦命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 初代[[宇佐国造]] | 主君 = [[神武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[宇佐氏]] | 父母 = 父：天三降命 | 兄弟 = 宇佐津媛命 | 妻 = | 子 = 常津彦命 | 特記事項 = }} '''菟狭津彦命'''（うさつひこのみこと、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[宇佐国造]]'''。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では'''宇沙都比古'''、『[[日本書紀]]』、「[[皇孫本紀]]」では'''菟狭津彦'''、「国造本紀」では'''宇佐都彦命'''、[[中臣氏|中臣氏族]]の各種系図<ref>[[中田憲信]]「赤堀」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>[[鈴木真年]]「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>中田憲信「兼松」『諸系譜』第二十四冊。</ref><ref name="keifu">中田憲信「荒木家系譜」『諸系譜』第三十三冊。</ref>では'''宇佐津彦命'''と表記される。
『古事記』では[[豊国]]の[[宇佐郡|宇沙]]に、『日本書紀』では[[筑紫国]]の菟狭にいた人物とされ、菟狭津媛命と共に兄妹で登場する。
『日本書紀』では、[[神武天皇]]が[[神武東征|東征]]し、中洲（[[大和国]]）をめざして[[国津神|国神]]の[[椎根津彦|珍彦]]を海導者として[[筑紫国]]の[[宇佐郡|菟狭]]に上陸したとき、[[宇佐国造|菟狭国造]]の祖である菟狭津彦と[[菟狭津媛]]がおり、[[駅館川|菟狭川]]の川上に一柱騰宮（あしひとつ あがりのみや）を造営して饗応の奉仕をした。そこで神武天皇は勅をして、妹の菟狭津媛を待臣の[[天種子命]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]遠祖）に娶らせたとされる。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』、『古事記』においても同様の伝承を記すが、『古事記』では天種子命に関する記述がない。
{{quote|宇佐郡 宇沙都彦命（忠君）（敬神）（大義名分）
[[高皇産霊尊|高皇産令命]]の孫[[天三降命|天御降命]]の子で宇佐島に座した。比売大神に奉仕したが 神武天皇東征の途次 宇佐に到り座せし一時 一柱騰宮を建てて御養を奉った。此れ即ち[[宇佐氏]]の祖で子孫は[[宮成氏|宮成]]、[[到津氏|到津]]両[[男爵]]家を始め数百家に反映してゐる。|『修身科郷土資料集成』{{sfn|大分県教育会|1936}}}}
== 系譜 == {{see |宇佐氏}} [[高皇産霊尊]]（たかみむすび）の孫・[[天三降命]]（[[天活玉命]]の子）の子とされるが、「宮成家譜」に天三降命は「天孫天降日向之時供奉」とあり、実際には天三降命は菟狭津彦命の祖父と見られる<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「第1章 天神系氏族 第11節 宇佐氏族 1宇佐公、宇佐宿祢」『古代氏族系譜集成』中巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年、978頁。</ref>。子に常津彦命がいるとされる。
妹の菟狭津媛命は[[天種子命]]の妻となり、[[中臣氏|中臣連]]の祖である[[宇佐津臣命]]を生んだ。ただし「荒木家系譜」では宇佐津媛命を宇佐津彦命の娘とする異説の載せる<ref name="keifu"/>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *{{anchors|大分県教育会}}{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県教育会| translator=| title=[{{NDLDC|918478/45}} 修身科郷土資料集成]| page=73| publisher=大分県教育会| location =大分県| year=1936| isbn=| ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[国造]]

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 菟狭津彦命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[上古#日本|上古]]、[[弥生時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 宇沙都比古、菟狭津彦、宇佐都彦命、宇佐津彦命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 初代[[宇佐国造]] | 主君 = [[神武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[宇佐氏]] | 父母 = | 兄弟 = 宇佐津媛命 | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''菟狭津彦命'''（うさつひこのみこと、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[宇佐国造]]'''。表記は「宇佐宿祢」系図などによる。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では'''宇沙都比古'''、『[[日本書紀]]』、「[[皇孫本紀]]」では'''菟狭津彦'''、「国造本紀」では'''宇佐都彦命'''、[[中臣氏|中臣氏族]]の各種系図<ref>[[中田憲信]]「赤堀」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>[[鈴木真年]]「中臣氏総系」『諸系譜』第三冊。</ref><ref>中田憲信「兼松」『諸系譜』第二十四冊。</ref><ref name="keifu">中田憲信「荒木家系譜」『諸系譜』第三十三冊。</ref>では'''宇佐津彦命'''と表記される。
『古事記』では[[豊国]]の[[宇佐郡|宇沙]]に、『日本書紀』では[[筑紫国]]の菟狭にいた人物とされ、菟狭津媛命と共に兄妹で登場する。
『日本書紀』では、[[神武天皇]]が[[神武東征|東征]]し、中洲（[[大和国]]）をめざして[[国津神|国神]]の[[椎根津彦|珍彦]]を海導者として[[筑紫国]]の[[宇佐郡|菟狭]]に上陸したとき、[[宇佐国造|菟狭国造]]の祖である菟狭津彦と[[菟狭津媛]]がおり、[[駅館川|菟狭川]]の川上に一柱騰宮（あしひとつ あがりのみや）を造営して饗応の奉仕をした。そこで神武天皇は勅をして、妹の菟狭津媛を待臣の[[天種子命]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]遠祖）に娶らせたとされる。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』、『古事記』においても同様の伝承を記すが、『古事記』では天種子命に関する記述がない。
{{quote|宇佐郡 宇沙都彦命（忠君）（敬神）（大義名分）
[[高皇産霊尊|高皇産令命]]の孫[[天三降命|天御降命]]の子で宇佐島に座した。比売大神に奉仕したが 神武天皇東征の途次 宇佐に到り座せし一時 一柱騰宮を建てて御養を奉った。此れ即ち[[宇佐氏]]の祖で子孫は[[宮成氏|宮成]]、[[到津氏|到津]]両[[男爵]]家を始め数百家に反映してゐる。|『修身科郷土資料集成』{{sfn|大分県教育会|1936}}}}
== 系譜 == {{see |宇佐氏}} [[高皇産霊尊]]（たかみむすび）の孫・[[天三降命]]（[[天活玉命]]の子）の子とされる。
妹の菟狭津媛命は[[天種子命]]の妻となり、[[中臣氏|中臣連]]の祖である[[宇佐津臣命]]を生んだ。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *{{anchors|大分県教育会}}{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県教育会| translator=| title=[{{NDLDC|918478/45}} 修身科郷土資料集成]| page=73| publisher=大分県教育会| location =大分県| year=1936| isbn=| ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[国造]]

妹の菟狭津媛命は[[天種子命]]の妻となり、[[中臣氏|中臣連]]の祖である[[宇佐津臣命]]を生んだ。

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 菟狭津彦命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[上古#日本|上古]]、[[弥生時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 =菟狭津彦、宇佐都彦命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 初代[[宇佐国造]] | 主君 = [[神武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[宇佐氏]] | 父母 = | 兄弟 = 宇佐津媛命 | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''菟狭津彦命'''（うさつひこのみこと、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[宇佐国造]]'''。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では'''宇沙都比古'''、『[[日本書紀]]』、「[[皇孫本紀]]」では'''菟狭津彦'''、「国造本紀」では'''宇佐都彦命'''と表記される。
『古事記』では[[豊国]]の[[宇佐郡|宇沙]]に、『日本書紀』では[[筑紫国]]の菟狭にいた人物とされ、菟狭津媛命と共に兄妹で登場する。
『日本書紀』では、[[神武天皇]]が[[神武東征|東征]]し、中洲（[[大和国]]）をめざして[[国津神|国神]]の[[椎根津彦|珍彦]]を海導者として[[筑紫国]]の[[宇佐郡|菟狭]]に上陸したとき、[[宇佐国造|菟狭国造]]の祖である菟狭津彦と[[菟狭津媛]]がおり、[[駅館川|菟狭川]]の川上に一柱騰宮（あしひとつ あがりのみや）を造営して饗応の奉仕をした。そこで神武天皇は勅をして、妹の菟狭津媛を待臣の[[天種子命]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]遠祖）に娶らせたとされる。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』、『古事記』においても同様の伝承を記すが、『古事記』では天種子命に関する記述がない。
{{quote|宇佐郡 宇沙都彦命（忠君）（敬神）（大義名分）
[[高皇産霊尊|高皇産令命]]の孫[[天三降命|天御降命]]の子で宇佐島に座した。比売大神に奉仕したが 神武天皇東征の途次 宇佐に到り座せし一時 一柱騰宮を建てて御養を奉った。此れ即ち[[宇佐氏]]の祖で子孫は[[宮成氏|宮成]]、[[到津氏|到津]]両[[男爵]]家を始め数百家に反映してゐる。|『修身科郷土資料集成』{{sfn|大分県教育会|1936}}}}
== 系譜 == {{see |宇佐氏}} [[高皇産霊尊]]（たかみむすび）の孫・[[天三降命]]（[[天活玉命]]の子）の子とされる。
妹の菟狭津媛命は[[天種子命]]の妻となり、[[中臣氏|中臣連]]の祖である[[宇佐津臣命]]を生んだ。

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古}}, {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} | lord = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}.
== Overview == In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛}}.
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, during the {{ill|Jimmu tosei|ja|神武東征}}, Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}. While similar traditions are recorded in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Notes ==
=== Annotations === {{notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}}

{{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古}}, {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} | lord = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}.
== Overview == In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛}}.
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, during the {{ill|Jimmu tosei|ja|神武東征}}, Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}. While similar traditions are recorded in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Notes ==
=== Annotations === {{notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}}
== References == *{{anchors|大分県教育会}}{{Cite book|author=大分県教育会|title=[{{NDLDC|918478/45}} 修身科郷土資料集成]|page=73|publisher=大分県教育会|location=大分県|year=1936|ref=harv}}

He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Notes ==
=== Annotations === {{notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古}}, {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} | lord = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}}.
== Overview == In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛}}.
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, during the {{ill|Jimmu tosei|ja|神武東征}}, Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}. While similar traditions are recorded in the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|ja|中臣氏}}.
== Notes ==
=== Annotations === {{notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}}
== References == *{{anchors|大分県教育会}}{{Cite book|author=大分県教育会|title=[{{NDLDC|918478/45}} 修身科郷土資料集成]|page=73|publisher=大分県教育会|location=大分県|year=1936|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 菟狭津彦命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[上古#日本|上古]]、[[弥生時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 =菟狭津彦、宇佐都彦命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 初代[[宇佐国造]] | 主君 = [[神武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[宇佐氏]] | 父母 = [[天三降命]] | 兄弟 = [[菟狭津媛|宇佐津媛命]] | 妻 = | 子 = 常津彦命 | 特記事項 = }} '''菟狭津彦命'''（うさつひこのみこと、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[宇佐国造]]'''。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では'''宇沙都比古'''、『[[日本書紀]]』、「[[皇孫本紀]]」では'''菟狭津彦'''、「国造本紀」では'''宇佐都彦命'''と表記される。
『古事記』では[[豊国]]の[[宇佐郡|宇沙]]に、『日本書紀』では[[筑紫国]]の菟狭にいた人物とされ、菟狭津媛命と共に兄妹で登場する。
『日本書紀』では、[[神武天皇]]が[[神武東征|東征]]し、中洲（[[大和国]]）をめざして[[国津神|国神]]の[[椎根津彦|珍彦]]を海導者として[[筑紫国]]の[[宇佐郡|菟狭]]に上陸したとき、[[宇佐国造|菟狭国造]]の祖である菟狭津彦と[[菟狭津媛]]がおり、[[駅館川|菟狭川]]の川上に一柱騰宮（あしひとつ あがりのみや）を造営して饗応の奉仕をした。そこで神武天皇は勅をして、妹の菟狭津媛を待臣の[[天種子命]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]遠祖）に娶らせたとされる。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』、『古事記』においても同様の伝承を記すが、『古事記』では天種子命に関する記述がない。
{{quote|宇佐郡 宇沙都彦命（忠君）（敬神）（大義名分）
[[高皇産霊尊|高皇産令命]]の孫[[天三降命|天御降命]]の子で宇佐島に座した。比売大神に奉仕したが 神武天皇東征の途次 宇佐に到り座せし一時 一柱騰宮を建てて御養を奉った。此れ即ち[[宇佐氏]]の祖で子孫は[[宮成氏|宮成]]、[[到津氏|到津]]両[[男爵]]家を始め数百家に反映してゐる。|『修身科郷土資料集成』{{sfn|大分県教育会|1936}}}}
== 系譜 == {{see |宇佐氏}} [[高皇産霊尊]]（たかみむすび）の孫・[[天三降命]]（[[天活玉命]]の子）の子とされる。
妹の菟狭津媛命は[[天種子命]]の妻となり、[[中臣氏|中臣連]]の祖である[[宇佐津臣命]]を生んだ。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *{{anchors|大分県教育会}}{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県教育会| translator=| title=[{{NDLDC|918478/45}} 修身科郷土資料集成]| page=73| publisher=大分県教育会| location =大分県| year=1936| isbn=| ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] * [[国造]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[菟狭津彦命]] to [[Usatsuhiko no Mikoto]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 菟狭津彦命 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[上古#日本|上古]]、[[弥生時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 =菟狭津彦、宇佐都彦命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 初代[[宇佐国造]] | 主君 = [[神武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[宇佐氏]] | 父母 = [[天三降命]] | 兄弟 = [[菟狭津媛|宇佐津媛命]] | 妻 = | 子 = 常津彦命 | 特記事項 = }} '''菟狭津彦命'''（うさつひこのみこと、[[生没年不詳]]）は、古代[[日本]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[宇佐国造]]'''。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』では'''宇沙都比古'''、『[[日本書紀]]』、「[[皇孫本紀]]」では'''菟狭津彦'''、「国造本紀」では'''宇佐都彦命'''と表記される。
『古事記』では[[豊国]]の[[宇佐郡|宇沙]]に、『日本書紀』では[[筑紫国]]の菟狭にいた人物とされ、菟狭津媛命と共に兄妹で登場する。
『日本書紀』では、[[神武天皇]]が[[神武東征|東征]]し、中洲（[[大和国]]）をめざして[[国津神|国神]]の[[椎根津彦|珍彦]]を海導者として[[筑紫国]]の[[宇佐郡|菟狭]]に上陸したとき、[[宇佐国造|菟狭国造]]の祖である菟狭津彦と[[菟狭津媛]]がおり、[[駅館川|菟狭川]]の川上に一柱騰宮（あしひとつ あがりのみや）を造営して饗応の奉仕をした。そこで神武天皇は勅をして、妹の菟狭津媛を待臣の[[天種子命]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]遠祖）に娶らせたとされる。『[[先代旧事本紀]]』、『古事記』においても同様の伝承を記すが、『古事記』では天種子命に関する記述がない。
{{quote|宇佐郡 宇沙都彦命（忠君）（敬神）（大義名分）
[[高皇産霊尊|高皇産令命]]の孫[[天三降命|天御降命]]の子で宇佐島に座した。比売大神に奉仕したが 神武天皇東征の途次 宇佐に到り座せし一時 一柱騰宮を建てて御養を奉った。此れ即ち[[宇佐氏]]の祖で子孫は[[宮成氏|宮成]]、[[到津氏|到津]]両[[男爵]]家を始め数百家に反映してゐる。|『修身科郷土資料集成』{{sfn|大分県教育会|1936}}}}
== 系譜 == {{see |宇佐氏}} [[高皇産霊尊]]（たかみむすび）の孫・[[天三降命]]（[[天活玉命]]の子）の子とされる。
妹の菟狭津媛命は[[天種子命]]の妻となり、[[中臣氏|中臣連]]の祖である[[宇佐津臣命]]を生んだ。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == *{{anchors|大分県教育会}}{{Cite book|和書|author=大分県教育会| translator=| title=[{{NDLDC|918478/45}} 修身科郷土資料集成]| page=73| publisher=大分県教育会| location =大分県| year=1936| isbn=| ref=harv}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110123233|qid=Q110123233}}|father={{ill|Q110120205|qid=Q110120205}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q109287983]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代|1=ancient history|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41493}}, [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lord = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Overview == In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsonki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko honki|qq=}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], during the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Jimmu tosei]], Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. While similar traditions are recorded in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Notes ==
=== Annotations === {{notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}}

There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110123233|WD=Q110123233}}|father={{ill|Q110120205|WD=Q110120205}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q109287983]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代|1=ancient history|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41493}}, [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lord = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Overview == In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsonki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko honki|qq=}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], during the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Jimmu tosei]], Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. While similar traditions are recorded in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110123233|WD=Q110123233}}|father={{ill|Q110120205|WD=Q110120205}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q109287983]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代|1=ancient history|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41493}}, [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lord = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Overview == In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsonki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko honki|qq=}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], during the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Jimmu tosei]], Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. While similar traditions are recorded in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110123233|WD=Q110123233}}|father={{ill|Q110120205|WD=Q110120205}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q109287983]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代|1=ancient history|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41493}}, [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lord = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Overview == In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsonki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko honki|qq=}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], during the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Jimmu tosei]], Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. While similar traditions are recorded in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].

[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Usa Clan]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]]<!--standardizing to enwiki standards where this would merely be the intersection of two categories-->[[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:People of Buzen Province]] [[Category:People of Hōshū (Toyokuni)]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q109287983}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110123233|WD=Q110123233}}|father={{ill|Q110120205|WD=Q110120205}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q109287983]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代|1=ancient history|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41493}}, [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lord = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Overview == In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsonki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko honki|qq=}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], during the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Jimmu tosei]], Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. While similar traditions are recorded in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].
== Notes ==
=== Annotations === {{notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}}
== References == *{{anchors|大分県教育会}}{{Cite book|author=大分県教育会|title=[{{NDLDC|918478/45}} 修身科郷土資料集成]|page=73|publisher=大分県教育会|location=大分県|year=1936|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|Kuni no miyatsuko]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110123233|WD=Q110123233}}|father={{ill|Q110120205|WD=Q110120205}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q109287983]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代|1=ancient history|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41493}}, [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lord = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Overview == In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsonki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko honki|qq=}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], during the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Jimmu tosei]], Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. While similar traditions are recorded in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110123233|WD=Q110123233}}|father={{ill|Q110120205|WD=Q110120205}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q109287983]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代|1=ancient history|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41493}}, [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lord = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Overview == In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsonki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko honki|qq=}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], during the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Jimmu tosei]], Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. While similar traditions are recorded in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110123233|WD=Q110123233}}|father={{ill|Q110120205|WD=Q110120205}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q109287983]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代|1=ancient history|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41493}}, [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lord = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Overview == In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsonki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko honki|qq=}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], during the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Jimmu tosei]], Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. While similar traditions are recorded in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110123233|WD=Q110123233}}|father={{ill|Q110120205|WD=Q110120205}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q109287983]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代|1=ancient history|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41493}}, [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lord = [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Overview == In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsonki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko honki|qq=}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], during the [[:en:Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|Jimmu tosei]], Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]]. While similar traditions are recorded in the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]].

== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}}.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110123233|WD=Q110123233}}|father={{ill|Q110120205|WD=Q110120205}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q109287983]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代|1=ancient history|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41493}}, {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lord = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Overview == In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsonki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko honki|qq=}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, during the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Jimmu tosei}}, Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}}. While similar traditions are recorded in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}}.
== Notes ==

== References == *{{anchors|大分県教育会}}{{Cite book|author=大分県教育会|title=[{{NDLDC|918478/45}} 修身科郷土資料集成]|page=73|publisher=大分県教育会|location=大分県|year=1936|ref=harv}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * {{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|en|Kuni no miyatsuko|WD=Q2483673|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko}}
