'''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsonki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko honki|qq=}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).

The {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, during the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Jimmu tosei}}, Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}}. While similar traditions are recorded in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.

{{quote|Usa District
Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)

A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}

== Genealogy ==
{{see|Usa clan}}
He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.

His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|children={{ill|Q110123233|WD=Q110123233}}|father={{ill|Q110120205|WD=Q110120205}}}} {{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代|1=ancient history|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41493}}, {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lord = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}} | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Overview == In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsonki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko honki|qq=}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, during the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Jimmu tosei}}, Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}}. While similar traditions are recorded in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:菟狭津彦命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Q110123233|WD=Q110123233|1=Tsunezuhiko no Mikoto}}|father={{ill|Q110120205|WD=Q110120205|1=Ame no Mikudaru}}}} {{Infobox noble | name = Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代|1=Ancient history|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41493}}, {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = Usatsuhiko, Usatsuhiko no Mikoto | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | titles = First {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | lord = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | house = {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}} | father = {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | siblings = {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | spouse = | issue = Tsunetsuhiko no Mikoto | notable_facts = }} '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Overview == In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsonki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko honki|qq=}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).
The {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, during the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Jimmu tosei|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}}, Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}}. While similar traditions are recorded in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.
{{quote|Usa District Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)
A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}
== Genealogy == {{see|Usa clan}} He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.
His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}}.
== Notes ==

'''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (菟狭津彦命, [[生没年不詳]]) was an ancient Japanese noble and the first {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, he is referred to as '''Usatsuhiko''' (宇沙都比古), in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} and {{ill|Kōsonki|ja|皇孫本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōsonki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as '''Usatsuhiko''' (菟狭津彦), and in the {{ill|Kujiki|lt=Kujiki|lt=Kuni no miyatsuko honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no miyatsuko honki|qq=}} as '''Usatsuhiko no Mikoto''' (宇佐都彦命).

The {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} describes him as residing in Usa in Toyokuni (豊国), while the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}} places him in Usa in Tsukushi (筑紫国), appearing alongside his sister {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, during the {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|WD=Q11590444|lt=Jimmu tosei|1=Jimmu's Eastern Expedition}}, Emperor Jimmu, aiming for Nakasu (present-day Yamato Province), landed in Usa, Tsukushi. There he encountered Usatsuhiko and Usatsuhime, ancestors of the {{ill|Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|宇佐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Kuni no miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} clan. They built a palace called Hitotsuhashi no Miya (一柱騰宮) on the upper reaches of the Usa River and hosted Emperor Jimmu. The emperor decreed that Usatsuhime be married to {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}}. While similar traditions are recorded in the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}} and {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} omits any mention of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}}.

Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)

A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}

== Genealogy ==
{{see|Usa clan}}
He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.

His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}}.

Usatsuhiko no Mikoto (loyalty, reverence to gods, moral principles)

A child of {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi}}’s grandson {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he resided on Usa Island. Serving the goddess {{ill|Hime Okami|ja|比売大神|lt=Hime-no-Ōkami|1=Himegami|3=比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime Okami|13=User:Immanuelle/Himegami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比売神}}, he hosted Emperor Jimmu during his eastern expedition and built Hitotsuhashi no Miya to provide for him. He is regarded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}, whose descendants include notable families like the Miyase and Itozu baronial families.|{{Cite book|title=修身科郷土資料集成|author=大分県教育会|year=1936}}}}

== Genealogy ==
{{see|Usa clan}}
He is considered a child of {{ill|Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja|天三降命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokushimori no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a descendant of {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|lt=Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takami Musubi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ|1=Takamimusubi}}. He was a member of the {{ill|Usa clan|ja|宇佐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa clan|qq=}}.

His sister, {{ill|Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja|菟狭津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsuhime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, became the wife of {{ill|Amenotaneko|lt=Ametaneko no Mikoto|ja|天種子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenotaneko|qq=}} and the mother of {{ill|Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja|宇佐津臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestor of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}}.
